objectives.concept: Enters global configuration mode on ArubaOS-CX.

objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Linux context, explain when to use 'ping' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows DNS resolver state, servers, and per-link DNS configuration on systemd-resolved systems.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Linux context, explain when to use 'resolvectl status' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Restarts the traditional Linux networking service on distributions that use it.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Linux context, explain when to use 'sudo systemctl restart networking' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Restarts the Linux NetworkManager service.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Linux context, explain when to use 'sudo systemctl restart NetworkManager' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Lists Linux TCP and UDP listening sockets with addresses, ports, and available process information.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Linux context, explain when to use 'ss -tulpn' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows whether the Linux NetworkManager service is loaded, active, failed, and its recent log messages.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Linux context, explain when to use 'systemctl status NetworkManager' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows hop-by-hop path toward a target.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Linux context, explain when to use 'traceroute' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows the local ARP cache mapping IP addresses to MAC addresses.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'arp -a' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Lists Windows files and directories in the current path.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'dir' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Scans the online Windows component store for corruption without repairing it.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'dism /online /cleanup-image /scanhealth' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Lists Windows network adapters, connection names, MAC addresses, and transport names.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'getmac /v' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows Windows network adapter IP address, subnet mask, default gateway, DNS servers, DHCP, and MAC details.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'ipconfig /all' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Releases the DHCP lease from Windows network adapters.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'ipconfig /release' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Requests a new DHCP lease for Windows network adapters.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'ipconfig /renew' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Disables a Windows network interface.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'netsh interface set interface <name> admin=disabled' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Enables a Windows network interface.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'netsh interface set interface <name> admin=enabled' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows Windows network interface administrative and connection state.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'netsh interface show interface' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows Windows IPv4 interfaces, indexes, metrics, MTU values, and connection states.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'netsh interface ipv4 show interfaces' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows wired LAN interface state and 802.1X-related information where available.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'netsh lan show interfaces' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Configures a static IPv4 address on a Windows interface.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'netsh interface ipv4 set address name=<name> static <ip> <mask> <gateway>' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Resets the Windows Winsock catalog.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'netsh winsock reset' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows the current Windows Wi-Fi adapter, connection, SSID, radio, channel, rates, and signal level.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'netsh wlan show interfaces' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Lists Windows TCP and UDP endpoints, connection states, listening ports, and owning process IDs.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'netstat -ano' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Queries DNS for hostname resolution and shows the DNS server used.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'nslookup' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Traces a Windows network path and measures packet loss at each hop over time.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'pathping <host>' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Tests basic reachability to an IP address or hostname using ICMP echo.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'ping' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows the Windows routing table and interface route metrics.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'route print' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows Windows operating system, hardware, boot time, memory, domain, and hotfix summary information.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'systeminfo' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.

objectives.concept: Shows hop-by-hop path toward a target using TTL-limited probes.
objectives.observable_student_behavior: Given Windows CMD context, explain when to use 'tracert' and identify the evidence or safe next action it supports.
